session.user:
Run these commands and report results:
1. `uv run ty check src/erk/cli/commands/pr/shared.py` 2. `uv run ruff check src/erk/cli/commands/pr/shared.py tests/unit/cli/commands/pr/test_shared.py` 3. `uv run ruff format --check src/erk/cli/commands/pr/shared.py tests/unit/cli/commands/pr/test_shared.py`
Report all results including any failures.
session.assistant: I'll execute these three commands and report the results.

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Run type checking on pr shared.py

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: uv run ruff check src/erk/cli/commands/pr/shared.py tests/unit/cli/commands/pr/test_shared.py
Run ruff linting on pr shared files

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: uv run ruff format --check src/erk/cli/commands/pr/shared.py tests/unit/cli/commands/pr/test_shared.py
Run ruff format check on pr shared files

session.tool_result:
Exit code 1 E501 Line too long (102 > 100) --> src/erk/cli/commands/pr/shared.py:313:101 | 311 | if plan_header is not None: 312 | # Draft PR: use original-plan format (from lifecycle module) 313 | pr_body_content = pr_body_content + build_original_plan_section(plan_context.plan_content) | ^^ 314 | else: 315 | # Issue-based: use existing format |
Found 1 error.
Exit code 1 Would reformat: src/erk/cli/commands/pr/shared.py 1 file would be reformatted, 1 file already formatted
session.assistant:
## Results Summary
**ty check passed** - All type checks passed!
**ruff check failed** - Found 1 linting violation: - `E501 Line too long (102 > 100)` at `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/pr/shared.py:313:101` - Line 313 exceeds the 100-character limit by 2 characters - Code: `pr_body_content = pr_body_content + build_original_plan_section(plan_context.plan_content)`
**ruff format check failed** - Formatting issues detected: - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/pr/shared.py` would be reformatted - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/unit/cli/commands/pr/test_shared.py` is already correctly formatted
The main issues are: 1. Line 313 in `shared.py` is 2 characters over the 100-character line limit 2. The file requires formatting to comply with ruff's formatting standards
